records.ety: [OE. ones, anes, an adverbial form fr. one, on, an, one. See One-, -Wards.]

defs: For one time; by limitation to the number one; not twice nor any number of times more than one.
quotes: Ye shall . . . go round about the city once.
Trees that bear mast are fruitful but once in two years.

defs: At some one period of time; -- used indefinitely.
quotes: My soul had once some foolish fondness for thee.
That court which we shall once govern.

defs: At any one time; -- often nearly equivalent to ever, if ever, or whenever; as, once kindled, it may not be quenched.
quotes: Wilt thou not be made clean? When shall it once be?
To be once in doubt Is once to be resolved.
notes: ☞ Once is used as a noun when preceded by this or that; as, this once, that once. It is also sometimes used elliptically, like an adjective, for once-existing. “The once province of Britain.” J. N. Pomeroy.
